skill.name: youtube-batch-transcript-extractor-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube transcripts and video metadata in batch through the BrowserAct API. The skill runs a Python helper that starts a BrowserAct workflow, polls for completion, and prints transcript results.
skill.summary: Batch extract YouTube transcripts and metadata with a BrowserAct API workflow.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The skill legitimately uses a Python script, the BrowserAct API, and a BROWSERACT_API_KEY to run a YouTube transcript workflow. I did not find evidence of malicious prompt injection, hidden exfiltration, or unauthorized local file access. Publication should include a warning that user search terms, workflow outputs, and the BrowserAct API credential are handled by a third-party service.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Receives User Query Data and Bearer Credential
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY, builds a Bearer Authorization header, and sends user search parameters to the BrowserAct workflow API. This is expected for the skill, but it exposes user queries and credential use to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly reads an API key and sends authenticated requests to BrowserAct. The behavior is intentional, so the concern is third-party data exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Credential Handling Guidance May Encourage Secret Sharing
medium_findings.description: The skill documentation and script tell the user to provide or configure the BrowserAct API key. This is a legitimate setup step, but marketplace users should be guided to use environment variables rather than paste secrets into chat.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence directly references the API key setup flow. The risk depends on user behavior, so it is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many SKILL.md backtick spans as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are Markdown formatting markers around parameter names, examples, and a documented Python invocation, not executable code inside SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is plainly Markdown documentation. The only real command is the intended Python helper invocation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns in SKILL.md, but I did not find cryptographic code or hash operations in the skill files. The referenced locations contain metadata, parameter descriptions, and output descriptions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic operation appears at the cited locations. The alerts appear to come from substring matches in normal documentation text.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Status and Error Checks
low_findings.description: The cited lines inspect API response content, task identifiers, status values, and documented error text. I did not find commands that enumerate the host system, processes, users, network interfaces, or local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code and text relate to remote workflow status and authorization errors. No host reconnaissance behavior is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Workflow Execution With Environment Credential
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines a local Python script, outbound HTTPS requests, and an environment-sourced API key. This combination is legitimate here, but it requires user trust in BrowserAct and careful secret handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combined pattern is present and security-relevant. Context shows it is the advertised function rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Researchers and analysts often need transcripts from many YouTube videos without building crawlers. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow that returns transcripts, links, publishers, and like counts.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python helper script for the BrowserAct YouTube transcript workflow.
Accepts a search keyword, upload date filter, and video limit.
Starts a remote BrowserAct workflow template through authenticated API requests.
Polls workflow status until completion, failure, cancellation, or timeout.
Prints transcript text and video metadata returned by the BrowserAct API.
Reports invalid authorization and connection errors to the user.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.
Depends on BrowserAct API availability and workflow behavior.
Only supports the upload date filter values documented by the skill.
May take several minutes because it waits for remote workflow completion.

use_cases.title: Build a video transcript dataset
use_cases.description: Collect transcripts and source metadata for a focused topic or time window.

use_cases.title: Monitor recent YouTube coverage
use_cases.description: Extract transcripts from recently uploaded videos about a product, event, or market.

use_cases.title: Prepare content for summarization
use_cases.description: Gather transcript text before using Claude, Codex, or Claude Code for analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Extract a small transcript sample
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to extract transcripts for 3 YouTube videos about renewable energy uploaded this week.

prompt_templates.title: Collect recent topic coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Find 5 YouTube videos about AI regulation uploaded this month and return transcripts with titles, URLs, publishers, and likes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a review dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract transcripts for 10 videos about electric vehicle battery recycling from this year. Include source links and channel names.

prompt_templates.title: Run and summarize a batch workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the batch transcript workflow for cybersecurity incident response videos from this week, then summarize recurring themes and cite source video URLs.

output_examples.input: Keyword: AI automation, upload date: This week, limit: 3
output_examples.output: A list of matching videos with each title, video URL, publisher, channel link, like count, and full transcript text.

output_examples.input: Keyword: climate policy, upload date: This month, limit: 5
output_examples.output: Transcript results suitable for research notes, including source metadata for each returned YouTube video.

output_examples.input: Missing or expired BrowserAct API key
output_examples.output: An error message explaining that authorization failed and that the BrowserAct API key must be checked.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable before running the skill.
Start with a small video limit to confirm the query and output format.
Review YouTube and BrowserAct terms before storing or redistributing transcript data.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys into shared chats, logs, or saved transcripts.
Do not request large batches before testing the workflow with a small limit.
Do not treat returned transcripts as verified facts without source review.

faq.question: What API key does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs a BrowserAct API key set in the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Does it download videos?
faq.answer: No. It requests transcript and metadata results from a BrowserAct workflow.

faq.question: What inputs can I provide?
faq.answer: You can provide a keyword, an upload date filter, and a maximum number of videos.

faq.question: How long does a run take?
faq.answer: The script can run for several minutes while the remote workflow completes.

faq.question: Can it analyze transcripts after extraction?
faq.answer: The skill extracts transcript data. You can then ask the agent to summarize or analyze the returned text.

faq.question: Is this safe for private research?
faq.answer: Use care. Search terms, workflow requests, and results are processed by BrowserAct as a third-party service.
